title: Conduct
content:
From the Mahabharata.

    Heed how thou livest. Do no act by day
    Which from the night shall drive thy peace away.
    In months of sun so live that months of rain
    Shall still be happy. Evermore restrain
    Evil and cherish good, so shall there be
    Another and a happier life for thee.
meaning:
This evocative piece by John Greenleaf Whittier, titled "Conduct", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
